name: Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar
name_original: Cañaveral desde Torrejoncillo – circular por el Castillo de Portezuelo y el Puente de Alconetar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Torrejoncillo traverse a landscape characterized by the Alagón river valley, offering a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents. The region features historical landmarks such as castles and ancient bridges, providing scenic backdrops for rides. Routes often pass through agricultural areas and open countryside, with some trails leading to nearby towns and villages. Best touring cycling routes around Torrejoncillo The most popular touring cycling route is Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This challenging route features historical sites and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Casas de Don Gómez from Coria – circular by Casillas de Coria, a moderate 15.6 miles (25.2 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding countryside and connects several small communities. Local touring cyclists also love the Galisteo from Coria – circular by Valle del río Alagón, a 36.4 miles (58.6 km) trail leading through the Alagón river valley, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Torrejoncillo is defined by river valleys, historical structures, and open agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Torrejoncillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrejoncillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torrejoncillo?
A: There are 18 touring cycling routes available around Torrejoncillo. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy rides to more challenging adventures, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while touring cycling around Torrejoncillo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Torrejoncillo traverse the picturesque Alagón river valley. You'll encounter a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents, passing through agricultural areas and open countryside. The region is also dotted with historical landmarks like castles and ancient bridges, providing scenic backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Torrejoncillo?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route around Torrejoncillo. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.9 miles (20.8 km) long and offers a gentle ride.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult touring cycling routes. One notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110714776/canaveral-from-torrejoncillo-circular-by-the-castillo-de-portezuelo-and-the-puente-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañaveral from Torrejoncillo – circular by the Castillo de Portezuelo and the Puente de Alconetar&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail featuring historical sites and varied terrain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Torrejoncillo are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111072740/casas-de-don-gomez-from-coria-circular-by-casillas-de-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Don Gómez from Coria – circular by Casillas de Coria&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110822232/galisteo-from-coria-circular-by-valle-del-rio-alagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo from Coria – circular by Valle del río Alagón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant historical landmarks. You can explore sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Portezuelo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;. These structures add a rich cultural dimension to your cycling experience.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions to look out for?
A: While specific viewpoints aren't highlighted, the routes frequently follow the Alagón river valley, offering picturesque views of the river and surrounding countryside. The agricultural landscapes and open fields also provide a serene and scenic environment for cycling.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I could find cafes?
A: Many routes are designed to connect several small communities and towns, such as Coria, Cañaveral, and Galisteo. These towns typically offer amenities like cafes and restaurants where you can take a break and refuel during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Torrejoncillo?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Torrejoncillo is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes around Torrejoncillo family-friendly?
A: While some routes are more challenging, there are moderate and easy options that can be suitable for families, especially those with some cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that could be a good starting point for families.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Torrejoncillo?
A: For routes starting directly in Torrejoncillo or nearby towns like Coria, you can typically find public parking areas within the town centers. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially in smaller villages.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling through the open countryside and along the Alagón river valley offers opportunities to observe local wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, especially near the river, and other small animals native to the Extremadura region.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Torrejoncillo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Torrejoncillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 3 reviews. Over 60 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty and the blend of historical and natural sights.
tips:
Portezuelo Castle, also known as Marmionda Castle, is an ancient fortress located in the municipality of Portezuelo, in the province of Cáceres.
The construction of the castle dates back to the 12th century. During this time, the Almohads invaded al-Andalus (1147), putting an end to the Almoravid empire. In 1167, the Christian king of León, Ferdinand II, conquered the castle and gave it to the Knights Templar. However, in 1196, the Almohads recovered it. Finally, in 1213, Alfonso IX of León definitively reconquered it and handed it over to the Order of San Julián del Pereiro, later known as the Order of Alcántara. Throughout its history, the castle underwent various renovations, the 16th century being the last of some importance. Unfortunately, it was abandoned in the second half of the 17th century.

Built over the old Vía de la Plata trade route that crossed the Tagus River during the Roman occupation of the peninsula in the 2nd century, the bridge is a unique historical monument for engineering.

Cañaveral is a Spanish town and municipality, located in the province of Cáceres, in the autonomous community of Extremadura. Administratively, it is part of the judicial district of Cáceres and the community of Riberos del Tajo. It is located in the center of the province, north of the Tagus River, near a chain of mountain ranges that extend from the Monfragüe National Park.
The fountain is located on one side of the track, ideal for a stop in the middle of the slope. There is a small shelter and picnic table by the fountain.
